title: Citrusy Filipino Pork Pancit Canton
description: A paleo-friendly Filipino dish featuring citrusy and indulgent flavors, made with vinegar and kombu-cold-extract, served in the form of pancit canton.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine rice flour and a pinch of salt, then gradually add in water to form a dough, kneading for about 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll out each piece into a long rope, then cut into thin strips to form the pancit canton noodles.
In a large pan, heat coconut oil over medium heat and stir-fry the pork shoulder until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add sliced onion and minced garlic to the pan and stir-fry until the onion is translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add vinegar, kombu-cold-extract, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to the pan, stirring to combine.
Add the cooked pancit canton noodles to the pan, stirring to combine with the pork and sauce mixture.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the noodles are well coated with the sauce.
Serve hot, garnished with sliced green onions and a sprinkle of salt and black pepper to taste.
